title: Project Foundation
summary: Complete foundation implementation with HTML structure, CSS grid layout, game state management, win/draw detection, localStorage integration, and comprehensive E2E test coverage (95/95 tests passing)
details: Created single HTML file with embedded CSS and JavaScript sections
Implemented CSS Grid 3x3 board layout with styled cells and dark theme
Added game state management with turn tracking, win detection (8 winning lines), and draw detection
Implemented cell click handlers with turn alternation and occupied cell protection
Added new game reset functionality
Integrated localStorage for score persistence with error handling
Added npm scripts for typecheck, build, lint, and test
Created comprehensive E2E test suite with Playwright (95 tests, 100% passing)
Verified all P0 requirements and edge cases handled

title: Single Player vs AI
summary: Complete AI opponent implementation with 3-button mode selection (Play as X, Play as O, Local). AI uses priority-based strategy: win > block > center > corner > any. Includes 300ms response delay, click guard protection, and comprehensive E2E test coverage (145/145 tests passing)
details: Added 3-button mode selection UI (Play as X vs AI, Play as O vs AI, vs Player local)
Extended game state with mode, playerSymbol, and aiSymbol tracking
Implemented AI logic with priority-based move selection (win/block/center/corner/any)
Added AI auto-response after player moves with 300ms delay for natural feel
Implemented click guard at top of handleCellClick to prevent player hijacking AI turn
Added AI first move logic in resetGame when player chooses O
Created 31 AI integration tests covering logic, game flow, and edge cases
Created 19 E2E user journey tests covering complete gameplay scenarios
Added Change Mode button for navigation back to mode selection
Updated all existing P0 tests to work with mode selection flow

title: Score Tracking
summary: Complete score tracking with separate single-player and multiplayer tracking, UI display, localStorage persistence, and reset functionality (186/186 tests passing - 100%)
details: Updated score structure with xWins/oWins for multiplayer mode (was wins/losses/draws)
Added score display HTML with mode-specific visibility (sp-scores for AI, mp-scores for local)
Added CSS styling with color-coded scores (green=wins, red=losses for SP; red=X, green=O for MP)
Implemented updateScoreDisplay() function called on mode changes and page load
Implemented recordScore() function integrated into game over detection in handleCellClick
Added Reset Scores button in controls area with resetScores() function
Score persistence via localStorage with error handling and structure migration
Comprehensive E2E test coverage with 23 integration tests covering all functionality
Created 18 E2E user journey tests covering 6 major user scenarios from user perspective
Fixed 4 localStorage persistence test regressions from score structure change
All 186 tests passing (100%) including 41 tests specifically for P2 Score Tracking

title: Animations
summary: Complete animation implementation with cell mark appearance, winning line highlights, score counter animations, and smooth board reset transitions. All 221 tests passing including 20 animation-specific E2E tests. Includes accessibility support for reduced motion preferences.
details: Added CSS animations for cell marks with scale and opacity transitions (0.2s duration)
Modified updateUI() to detect new marks and apply animate-in class
Added @keyframes winningPulse with background and box-shadow pulse animation (0.8s, 3 iterations)
Created getWinningLine() function to retrieve winning cell indices without modifying checkWinner()
Created highlightWinningLine() function to apply winning class to cells
Modified handleCellClick() to highlight winning lines on game completion
Modified resetGame() to clear winning classes from all cells
Added @keyframes scorePop with scale animation (0.3s duration)
Created animateScoreUpdate() function with animationend event cleanup
Rewrote recordScore() function with complete element ID mapping for all score scenarios
Added reduced motion support via @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) for all animations
Expanded e2e/animations.spec.js from 1 to 13 comprehensive tests
Fixed bug where updateUI() was overwriting winning class by preserving it during className updates
Added @keyframes cellClear animation (opacity 1→0, scale 1→0.8, 150ms) for smooth board reset
Modified _resetGameState() with animate parameter for two-phase reset strategy (animated vs immediate)
Implemented immediate state reset with delayed UI update to prevent unplayable period during animation
Early guard release in _resetGameState() to allow rapid resets without blocking legitimate user actions
Updated resetGame() with 450ms AI delay (150ms animation + 300ms thinking) for proper timing
Updated game initialization functions (startLocalGame, startAIGame, showModeSelection) to use immediate reset
Added 7 E2E tests for board reset transitions covering animation, rapid resets, AI integration, and WebRTC sync
Modified existing test to verify 150ms animation timing instead of instant clearing
All 221 E2E tests passing (100%) including 20 animation-specific tests

title: Peer-to-Peer Multiplayer
summary: WebRTC peer-to-peer multiplayer with manual signaling, move/reset sync, disconnection handling (205/205 tests passing - 100%)
details: Added Play Online mode button with WebRTC browser detection
Connection panel for offer/answer code exchange via base64-encoded codes
WebRTC connection with STUN server (stun.l.google.com:19302)
Data channel for move and reset message sync between peers
Turn enforcement for remote play (block clicks when not your turn)
Modified score functions to handle webrtc mode (else instead of === local)
Graceful disconnection handling with cleanup and status messages
6 E2E tests with two-context pattern for peer-to-peer simulation
All async operations wrapped in try-catch with error handlers
Host is always X, Guest is always O with automatic assignment
Internal _resetGameState() function to prevent reset message loops
Copy to clipboard functionality with fallback to text selection

title: Disappearing Marks Mechanic
summary: Dynamic gameplay mechanic where the oldest mark disappears after 5 total moves are placed, limiting the board to 5 marks at any time
